column1: Summer Camp - Rock & Ropes 1 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 focused on introduction to rock climbing. Campers learn to belay, tie climbing knots, rappel, prusik, and try basic anchor work. Each group gets one outdoor climbing field trip during the week to Exit 38 near North Bend. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park for a hike, picnic, and beach time. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Thursday. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Water Week 1 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 with a focus on water skills and safety. Campers try river paddling on a guided river float field trip (Tuesday or Wednesday), tide pooling, climbing, hiking, navigation, first aid, and knots. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Thursday. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Climbing Rock Stars - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 focused on advancing climbing skills. In addition to top-roping, belaying, and rappelling, campers practice clipping draws, mock-leading, anchor building, and some may lead sport routes. Each group gets one outdoor climbing field trip to Exit 38 near North Bend. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Thursday. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Nature Arts & Crafts Week 1 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 connecting outdoor skills with arts and crafts. Wednesday and Thursday are field trip days with an easy hike in the Mt. Rainier area focused on nature photography and watercolor painting. Activities may include climbing, rope skills, navigation, wilderness first aid, drawing, mural creation, 3D topographic maps, ropes-based crafts, paper mache, tie-dye, and pyrography. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Rock & Ropes 2 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 focused on introduction to rock climbing. Identical curriculum to Rock & Ropes 1, simply the second instance of the theme. Campers learn to belay, tie knots, rappel, prusik, and try basic anchor work. Each group gets one outdoor climbing field trip to Exit 38 near North Bend. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Thursday. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Water Week 2 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 with a focus on water skills and safety. This week each camper gets a kayaking field trip on either Wednesday or Thursday. Campers also try river paddling, tide pooling, climbing, hiking, navigation, first aid, and knots. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm Monday through Thursday. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Teen Adventures: Rock Climbing - Tacoma
column11: Four-day teen rock climbing adventure for ages 13-17, all experience levels welcome. Tuesday is at the Tacoma Program Center for climbing basics including top-roping, belaying, rappelling, knots, and anchors. Wednesday through Friday is outdoor climbing at Exit 38 and Exit 32 with two nights of camping nearby. Skills covered may include lead belaying, mock-leading, leading on bolts, anchor building, and rescue skills. Participants may hike up to 1 mile with 30-pound packs. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Nature Arts & Crafts Week 2 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 connecting outdoor skills with arts and crafts. Wednesday and Thursday are field trip days with an easy hike in the Mt. Rainier area focused on nature photography and watercolor painting. Activities may include climbing, rope skills, navigation, wilderness first aid, drawing, mural creation, 3D topographic maps, ropes-based crafts, paper mache, tie-dye, and pyrography. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Climbing Rock Stars 2 - Tacoma
column11: Five-day Tacoma day camp at the Mountaineers Tacoma Program Center for kids in grades 1-7 focused on advancing climbing skills. Identical curriculum to Climbing Rock Stars 1, simply the second instance of the theme. Activities include top-roping, belaying, rappelling, clipping draws, mock-leading, anchor building, and possibly leading sport routes for ready students. Each group gets one outdoor climbing field trip to Exit 38 near North Bend. Friday meets at Point Defiance Park. Camp runs 8:30am to 4:30pm. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Summer Camp - Teen Adventures: Backpacking - Tacoma
column11: Four-day teen backpacking adventure for ages 13-17, new and experienced backpackers welcome. Tuesday is at the Tacoma Program Center learning backcountry skills including first aid, navigation, the 10 essentials, Leave No Trace principles, and using backpacking gear. Wednesday through Friday is a multi-day backpacking trip in the wilderness covering 5-7 miles per day with 1,000-2,000 feet of elevation gain, carrying packs that weigh 30-35 pounds. Led by Sarah Holt.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 1: Animal Life
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 1 themed Animal Life with trips to Kopachuck wildlife area, Northwest Trek Wildlife Park, Woodland Park Zoo, Tahoma Alpaca Mill, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper. Campers should bring lunch, water bottle, walking shoes, Bible, and swim suit when needed.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 2: Super Cool
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 2 themed Super Cool with trips to USS Turner Joy, Gig Harbor Air Museum, Undersea Naval Museum, and Wild Waves Theme & Water Park. No camp on July 3. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 3: Build It Yourself
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 3 themed Build It Yourself with trips to LeMay Auto Museum, kite building and flying, bike riding, a box car team challenge, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 4: Sweets
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 4 themed Sweets with an ice cream taste challenge, Seattle Chocolate Factory, donuts in the South Sound, Boehm Chocolate Factory, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 5: Mariners
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 5 themed Mariners with ice blocking, boating and tubing days, a Mariners baseball game, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 6: Adventure
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 6 themed Adventure with ice skating, canoeing at Sunset Lake, blueberry picking, the Olympia Farmers Market, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Sound Life Day Camp - Week 7: Aquatics
column11: Tacoma Christian day camp with daily off-site field trips. Week 7 themed Aquatics with multiple boating and tubing days, a Pike Place Market trip, and Friday swimming. Camp hours 8:15am to 4:30pm with free before-care 7:30 to 8:15am and aftercare 4:30 to 5:30pm. One-time $30 registration fee per camper.

column1: Wolf Camp Puyallup - Wilderness Survival Craft
column11: Hands-on wilderness survival day camp at Clark's Creek Park South Shelter and the Blue Skye Farm. Campers practice fire-building, shelter construction, knife and tool skills, and basic outdoor survival. Daily schedule runs 9:00am to 3:45pm with morning lessons, lunch with games and archery, and afternoon activities. No prerequisites required. 6:1 student-teacher ratio.

column1: Wolf Camp Puyallup - Wildlife Search & Rescue
column11: Wildlife tracking and search and rescue day camp at Clark's Creek Park South Shelter and Blue Skye Farm. Campers hike with day packs, learn to read animal tracks and bird signs, and follow lessons that adapt to where the wildlife leads. No prerequisites required. 6:1 student-teacher ratio. Daily schedule 9:00am to 3:45pm with morning lessons, lunch with games and archery, and afternoon activities.

column1: Wolf Camp Puyallup - Wild Cooking & Herbology
column11: Wild edible plants and outdoor cooking day camp at Clark's Creek Park South Shelter and Blue Skye Farm in Puyallup. Campers identify edible and medicinal plants, prepare wild foods over campfires, and learn herbology basics. Best for campers focused in group learning environments or returning students. Daily schedule 9:00am to 3:45pm. 6:1 student-teacher ratio.

column1: Wolf Camp Puyallup - Intro & Advanced Wilderness Survival
column11: Combined intro and advanced wilderness survival day camp running concurrently at Clark's Creek Park South Shelter and Blue Skye Farm. Campers without prerequisites join the intro track while returning campers can take the advanced wilderness survival track. Daily schedule 9:00am to 3:45pm with morning lessons, lunch with games and archery, and afternoon activities. 6:1 student-teacher ratio.

column1: Wolf Camp Puyallup - Advanced Herbal Medicine, Cooking & Craft
column11: Advanced herbal medicine, wild cooking, and traditional craft day camp at Clark's Creek Park South Shelter and Blue Skye Farm in Puyallup. Prerequisite is the Wild Cooking & Herbology week, the family ethnobotany workshop, or two prior day camps or family workshops. Daily schedule 9:00am to 3:45pm. 6:1 student-teacher ratio.

column1: i9 Sports Puyallup - Soccer League Summer 2026
column11: Co-ed Sunday youth soccer league at Rogers-Ram Field, the all-weather turf field at Rogers High School in South Hill. Six-week schedule including opening day and playoffs. Practices held on game day prior to the game. No tryouts or drafts. Pee Wee 3-6 plays 4v4 or 5v5 no goalie, Junior 7-9 plays 6v6 or 7v7 with goalie, Senior 10+ plays 6v6 or 7v7 with goalie. Reversible jersey included in fee. Shin guards required. Run by League Office 426.
column23: Sundays, practice on game day prior to game; ages 3-6 between 9am-2pm, ages 7-11 between 11am-5pm

column1: Breakthrough Basketball - Ball Handling and Scoring Skills Camp Tacoma
column11: Three-day youth basketball camp at Bellarmine Preparatory School for boys and girls in 3rd through 8th grade. Focus on developing scoring moves, ball handling, passing, athleticism, footwork, and 1-on-1 moves. Each session is limited to 60 players grouped by grade and gender during individual skill drills. Camp runs 9:00am to 3:00pm Tuesday through Thursday.

column1: Breakthrough Basketball - Elite Skills & Playmaking Camp Auburn
column11: Three-day elite youth basketball camp at Auburn Fieldhouse USA for boys and girls in 7th through 12th grade. Focus on advanced skills and playmaking ability with explosive dribble moves, scoring, ball handling, passing, athleticism, footwork, and decision-making. Limited to 60 players. Camp runs 9:00am to 3:00pm Tuesday through Thursday. Instructor Corey Laster.

column1: Breakthrough Basketball - Ball Handling and Scoring Skills Camp Auburn
column11: Three-day youth basketball camp at Auburn Fieldhouse USA for boys and girls in 3rd through 8th grade. Focus on scoring moves, ball handling, passing, footwork, and 1-on-1 moves. Limited to 60 players grouped by grade and gender. Camp runs 9:00am to 3:00pm Tuesday through Thursday. Instructor Shawn Carson.

column1: Breakthrough Basketball - Essential Skills Camp Auburn
column11: Three-day youth basketball camp at Auburn Fieldhouse USA for boys and girls in 3rd through 8th grade. Focus on essential basketball skills including shooting, ball handling, passing, footwork, and decision-making. Limited to 60 players grouped by grade and gender. Camp runs 9:00am to 3:00pm Tuesday through Thursday. Instructor Martin Anderson.

column1: Breakthrough Basketball - Attack & Counter Camp Auburn
column11: Three-day youth basketball camp at Auburn Fieldhouse USA for boys and girls in 7th through 12th grade. Focus on attack and counter offensive skills. Limited to 60 players. Camp runs 9:00am to 3:00pm Monday through Wednesday. Instructor Damion Byrd.
